rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) What happened to the GMC truck? (2) It is unclear why Executor mentions the 100 shares of SPDR S&P that were sold during the first accounting period and correctly shown with the I&A value of $6,601.78 in the amendment filed on 5/5/22 (3) It would be helpful if the sale price of the real property sold on 8/17/23 were reported, even if the sale won't be reported until the next accounting. Also, if the executor reimbursed the estate for the $1,749.00 he inadvertently paid out of the estate instead of from his own bank account, proof of such reimbursement should be filed promptly even if the amount will not be reported until the next accounting. (4) A personal representative with even limited IAEA authority (which only limits the sale of real property) has the power to distribute tangible personal property after the time for filing creditor claims has expired and after giving notice of proposed action to the persons entitled to the property under the decedent's will, not to exceed an aggregate FMV of $50,000. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 3/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARTHUR B. SKINNER JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (3) A report of status even without an accounting ordinarily discloses the amount of proceeds from the sale of any estate assets. (The creditor claims period expired on 3/17/22, and the notices to the DHCS and FTB were presumably also mailed in January 2022) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Unless all heirs waive an accounting, an accounting for the first two years of estate administration is already delinquent. [LRSF 14.34 A.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SU HWA WAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To (1) Compel Arbitration, (2) Dismiss Or Stay Claims Against Fidelity, And (3) For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondents' Motion to (1) Compel Arbitration, (2) Dismiss or Stay Claims Agaisnt Fidelity, and (3) for Attorney's Fees. Respondents' Motion to (1) Compel Arbitration, (2) Dismiss or Stay Claims Agaisnt Fidelity, and (3) for Attorney's Fees is DENIED AS MOOT, in light of the concurrently issued tentative ruling granting Petitioner's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SU HWA WAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition. The Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition is brought pursuant to CCP section 576 and under various case law. Under CCP section 576, "Any judge, at any time before or after commencement of trial, in the furtherance of justice, and upon such terms as may be proper, may allow the amendment of any pleading[.]" Petitioner seeks leave to file a Second Amended Petition, submitted as Exhibit A to the Declaration of Ciaran O'Sullivan. The proposed Second Amended Petition would add two new Respondents and three new causes of action based on facts alleged in the First Amended Petition to Confirm That Decedent's Fidelity Accounts Are Estate Property; For Instructions; For Injunction Barring Fidelity, or Any Affiliated Entity From Distributing Funds to Itself, filed July 31, 2023. Current Respondents Fidelity Brokerage Services, LLC and Fidelity Investments filed Notice of Non-Opposition to Petitioners Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition. Current Respondent Fidelity Investments Charitable Gift Fund separately filed Statement of Non-Opposition to Petitioners Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition. Judicial policy strongly favors allowing a party to further amend a pleading, absent a showing of prejudice to responding parties, sham pleading, or uncurable legal defect. The court does not find a showing of any of these exceptions on the current record. In light of these considerations, and the current Respondents' non-opposition to the Motion, Petitioner's Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition is GRANTED. Petitioners shall file and serve the Second Amended Petition, as submitted with the Motion for Leave to Amend, within 7 days of entry of this order. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The parties to report to the court with the status of the proceeding. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MICHAEL J. AND BARBARA A. RUSSELL 2002 TRUST, dated december 27, 2002 as amended and restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For (1) Instructions To Trustee, (2) To Enjoin Sale Of Trust Property Pending, And (3) For A Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAYDEN CHEUKHEI KAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered by the petitioner in a verified supplement to his petition. (2) Neither proof of service is complete at item 4b. (3) The service list has addresses that appear not to be the correct name of the street. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/23/24. (No courtesy copy of the petition or any papers other than the two incomplete proofs of service were received by the examiner.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
